summary: Defines the Source interface and SourceMessage struct — the common contract for all content sources

guarantees: FetchMessages returns (messages, newCheckpoint, error) — checkpoint is opaque string, format is implementation-defined
Empty checkpoint means initial fetch; non-empty checkpoint means incremental sync
SourceMessage fields Title, URL, Author are optional (empty string is valid)

observations.text: Checkpoint is deliberately opaque (string) — each implementation encodes its own cursor format (integer ID for HN/Telegram, UTC timestamp for Reddit, RFC3339+ETag+Last-Modified for RSS)
SourceMessage.ExternalID must be unique within the source — callers use it for deduplication. No enforcement here; implementations are responsible.
